title: Create Three.js Viewport
prompt:
# TASK 1: Create Three.js Viewport

## CONTEXT
**Current state:** Empty Electron app. index.html has only TODO comments, no implementation.
**Read first:** ClaudeCAD-Specification.md sections 2.4 (File Structure), 3.2 (Viewport), Appendix B (Colors)

## OBJECTIVE
Create the Three.js viewport from scratch with proper file structure.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Create demo/ directory
2. Create src/renderer/styles.css with dark theme base styles
3. Create src/renderer/renderer.js as ES module that:
   - Imports Three.js
   - Creates scene with dark background (#1e1e1e)
   - Creates PerspectiveCamera
   - Creates WebGLRenderer
   - Adds GridHelper on XY plane (Z-up)
   - Adds AxesHelper at origin
   - Adds test cube (temporary, will remove later)
   - Adds ambient and directional lights
   - Runs animation loop
   - Handles window resize
4. Update index.html to link CSS and import renderer.js module

## VERIFY
Run npm start:
- Window opens with dark background
- Grid visible (horizontal)
- Colored axes visible (RGB = XYZ)
- Blue cube visible
- Console shows success message
- No errors

## NEXT TASK
Add OrbitControls for camera navigation

title: Add OrbitControls
prompt:
# TASK 2: Add OrbitControls

## CONTEXT
**Current state:** Static Three.js viewport with grid, axes, cube. No camera controls yet.
**Read first:** Three.js OrbitControls documentation, spec section 3.2.2

## OBJECTIVE
Add Three.js OrbitControls for camera navigation.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Import OrbitControls from Three.js addons/examples
2. Create OrbitControls with damping enabled (factor 0.05)
3. Configure mouse buttons: left = rotate, right = pan, scroll = zoom
4. Set camera up vector to (0,0,1) for Z-up orientation
5. Set initial camera position for isometric-like view (e.g., 70, -70, 50)
6. Call controls.update() in animation loop

## VERIFY
Run npm start:
- Left mouse drag rotates view around origin
- Right mouse drag pans the view
- Scroll wheel zooms in/out
- Movement is smooth with damping
- Z-axis (blue) points UP

## NEXT TASK
Add header bar with View dropdown

title: Add Header with View Dropdown
prompt:
# TASK 3: Add Header with View Dropdown

## CONTEXT
**Current state:** Full-window viewport with OrbitControls working.
**Read first:** ClaudeCAD-Specification.md section 3.1 (Layout) and 3.2.2 (View Controls), Appendix B

## OBJECTIVE
Add header bar with app title and view preset dropdown.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Wrap content in #app container with flexbox column layout
2. Add #header div (32px height) containing:
   - App title 'ClaudeCAD' on left (muted color)
   - View dropdown on right with options: Isometric (default), Front, Back, Top, Bottom, Left, Right
3. Style header with dark theme colors from Appendix B
4. Update renderer.js to size canvas to #viewport div, not window
5. Define camera positions for each view preset
6. Handle dropdown change to snap camera to selected position

## VERIFY
Run npm start:
- Header bar visible at top with title and dropdown
- Selecting 'Top' shows view looking straight down at grid
- Selecting 'Front' shows view from Y-axis direction  
- Selecting 'Isometric' restores angled view
- OrbitControls still work after changing preset

## NEXT TASK
Add chat panel and status bar

title: Add Chat Panel and Status Bar
prompt:
# TASK 4: Add Chat Panel and Status Bar

## CONTEXT
**Current state:** Header with dropdown, viewport fills rest of window.
**Read first:** ClaudeCAD-Specification.md sections 3.1, 3.5, 3.6, Appendix B

## OBJECTIVE
Add chat panel at bottom and status bar at very bottom.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Add #chat-panel div after viewport containing:
   - Empty message display area (scrollable)
   - Input container with textarea and Send button
   - Fixed height ~180px
2. Add #status-bar div at very bottom containing:
   - Left: status text showing 'Ready'
   - Right: placeholder span for model stats
   - Height 24px
3. Style with dark theme colors from Appendix B
4. Use flexbox so viewport takes remaining space between header and chat
5. Use ResizeObserver in renderer.js for viewport sizing

## VERIFY
Run npm start:
- Layout top to bottom: header, viewport, chat panel, status bar
- Chat has message area and input with Send button
- Status bar shows 'Ready' on left
- Viewport fills available space, not squashed
- All colors match dark theme

## NEXT TASK
Make chat panel resizable

title: Make Chat Panel Resizable
prompt:
# TASK 5: Make Chat Panel Resizable

## CONTEXT
**Current state:** Chat panel has fixed 180px height.
**Read first:** ClaudeCAD-Specification.md section 3.5.1

## OBJECTIVE
Allow user to resize chat panel by dragging its top edge.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Add resize handle div at top of chat panel (4px tall)
2. Style handle: transparent normally, accent color (#4a9eff) on hover, ns-resize cursor
3. Implement drag-to-resize in renderer.js:
   - mousedown on handle: start tracking
   - mousemove: calculate new height from mouse Y
   - mouseup: stop tracking
4. Enforce constraints: minimum 100px, maximum 60% of window height
5. Prevent text selection during drag (set userSelect none)
6. Viewport resizes automatically via ResizeObserver

## VERIFY
Run npm start:
- Cursor changes to resize cursor on chat top edge
- Handle highlights blue on hover
- Drag up = chat taller
- Drag down = chat shorter
- Minimum and maximum constraints work
- Viewport resizes correctly, no distortion

## NEXT TASK
Add loading spinner overlay

title: Add Loading Spinner
prompt:
# TASK 6: Add Loading Spinner

## CONTEXT
**Current state:** Full layout with resizable chat panel.
**Read first:** ClaudeCAD-Specification.md section 3.2.4, Appendix B

## OBJECTIVE
Add loading spinner overlay on viewport for AI generation feedback.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Add overlay div inside #viewport (hidden by default with CSS class)
2. Overlay: semi-transparent dark background rgba(30,30,30,0.8), covers viewport
3. Spinner: CSS-only rotating ring, accent color (#4a9eff), ~48px, centered
4. Create showLoading() and hideLoading() functions, expose on window object
5. showLoading: remove hidden class, set status text to 'Generating...'
6. hideLoading: add hidden class, set status text to 'Ready'
7. Add temporary 'L' key listener to toggle for testing

## VERIFY
Run npm start:
- Press L key: spinner appears centered over viewport
- Spinner rotates smoothly (CSS animation)
- Scene visible but dimmed behind overlay
- Status bar shows 'Generating...'
- Press L again: spinner hides, status shows 'Ready'
- Overlay only covers viewport, not header/chat/status

## NEXT TASK
Remove test cube and finalize

title: Remove Test Cube and Finalize
prompt:
# TASK 7: Remove Test Cube and Finalize

## CONTEXT
**Current state:** Full Phase 1 UI complete, test cube still in scene.

## OBJECTIVE
Remove test geometry and prepare for Phase 2.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Remove test cube code from renderer.js (geometry, material, mesh, scene.add)
2. Keep the lighting (ambient + directional lights)
3. Add placeholder: let currentMesh = null;
4. Add placeholder function loadMesh(path) that logs 'loadMesh not implemented' and expose on window
5. Change console log to 'ClaudeCAD Phase 1 Complete'

## VERIFY
Run npm start:
- NO cube visible - only empty grid and axes
- Grid horizontal on XY plane
- Z-axis (blue) points UP
- Header with View dropdown - all presets work
- Chat panel with input, resizable
- Status bar shows 'Ready'
- L key toggles loading spinner
- Console shows 'ClaudeCAD Phase 1 Complete'
- No errors

## PHASE 1 COMPLETE
Ready for Phase 2: Python Backend
